title: Sensing and control arrangements for respiratory device
abstract: Various characteristics of a gas flow can be sensed at the end of a respiratory conduit near the patient interface using a sensing module. The sensing module can be removable from the patient end of the respiratory conduit for ease of use and ease of cleaning. The sensor module can transmit sensor data over the same wires used to heat the respiratory conduit.
claims: 1. A connector adapted to be positioned between components of a respiratory therapy system, the connector comprising: a body comprising: a gases inlet, a gases outlet, and a lumen extending between the gases inlet and the gases outlet, and an input device comprising a depressible member adapted to be actuated by a user, the input device located on the body, wherein the input device is adapted to transmit an input signal to control at least one component of the respiratory therapy system, wherein the input device is configured to transmit a first input signal when the input device is actuated in a first manner and to transmit a second input signal when the input device is actuated in a second manner.
